it:72.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:72.3:
"There are these three properties for escape. Which three? This is the escape from sensuality: renunciation.
This is the escape from form: formlessness. And as for whatever has come into being, is fabricated and dependently co-arisen, the escape from that is cessation. These are the three properties for escape."
Knowing the escape from sensuality, and the overcoming of forms — ardent always — touching the stilling of all fabrications:
it:72.4: He is a monk who's seen rightly. From that he is there set free. A master of direct knowing, at peace, he is a sage gone beyond bonds.
it:72.5: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
